paths.label: Hard Full Closure
paths.summary: Close both capability and accountability surfaces as much as possible.
advantages: Maximum control over internal capability surfaces
paths.judgment: Too brittle. Safety gains are offset by accountability loss.

paths.label: Closed Core, Accountable Membrane
paths.summary: Keep recursive capability and novelty generation internal while exposing bounded products and a visible accountability surface.
advantages: Maintains institutional legitimacy through auditable doctrine
Supports revenue through products, services, and selective licensing
costs: Requires major rewrites in governance, economics, and public narrative
Demands real release-review and refusal infrastructure
paths.judgment: Recommended. Best fit for the operator's intent and the strongest salvage path from the current architecture.

paths.label: Open Platform With Stronger Filters
paths.summary: Keep marketplace and broad platform posture, but add policy filters and abuse controls.
costs: Does not solve the operator's core concern about capability diffusion
Leaves raw novelty and misuse surfaces too exposed
paths.judgment: Rejected. Misaligned with the stated mission pivot.

selected_path.reason: It preserves the operator's requirement for a closed capability core while avoiding the governance and legitimacy failure mode of total opacity.

execution_stages.title: Economic and product membrane rewrite
outcomes: Licensing and invention brokerage rules defined

execution_stages.title: Native model sovereignty program
outcomes: AASL and AACP data/control planes stabilized
Trace corpus and eval program created
